title: rosa communis var. tomentella
intro: Rosa communis var. tomentella, also known as the hairy dog rose, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rosaceae. The hairy dog rose has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids, which have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. The hairy dog rose can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
